title: Graphardisia picturata
taxonomy: Graphardisia picturata is classified under the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family includes over 2,000 species of trees and shrubs, many of which produce edible fruits. Graphardisia picturata is a tropical plant native to Central and South America. It is a member of the genus Graphardisia, which is a relatively small genus within the Annonaceae family. The classification of Graphardisia picturata is further detailed at the species level, where it is considered a distinct species within the broader family.
uses: Graphardisia picturata is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, and its medicinal uses are still being researched.
